patches.hypothesis: The original patch narrowly addresses the IE bug by only marking functions with exactly two parameters as escaped. To generalize this fix, we should mark all parameters of functions as escaped, regardless of their count, to prevent writing to them causing exceptions in IE.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because the code does not correctly handle the special case for functions with exactly two parameters due to an IE bug. The previous attempts failed due to incorrect method usage or compilation errors. We need to modify the code to mark the parameters as escaped without using prohibited methods.

patches.hypothesis: The issue arises because the method stops processing when it encounters an annotation. It should continue reading until it reaches an end-of-comment (EOC) or end-of-file (EOF) to capture the entire license block including annotations.

The issue arises because the method stops reading text when it encounters an annotation. The fix involves modifying the logic to ensure that annotations within a license block are included in the extracted text.

The original patch only addressed the specific issue of appending the remaining line after an EOL token without considering the broader context of handling multiple lines and annotations. The refined fix will ensure that all text between EOL tokens is correctly appended until an annotation, EOC, or EOF is encountered, and will handle whitespace options appropriately.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method incorrectly handling the transition from one line to another when encountering an annotation. Specifically, the code does not append the text following an EOL token correctly before reaching an annotation.

patches.hypothesis: The issue arises because the method stops processing when it encounters an annotation, even if it's part of a license block. We need to ensure that annotations within a license block are captured.
